intro: Hieracium linguans, also known as Meadow Hawkweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is commonly found in wet meadows and along streams. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium linguans is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and psoriasis.
